methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Focused Energy, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Thomas Forner and Markus Roth, operates within a fragmented sector comprising 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, Oklo, and X-Energy. The company has raised $80M+ to date.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to peer set constraints. Stage-Progression methodology, anchored to the $1.0B total raised proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $2.0B. A peer-median approach could not be applied; while 8 same-sector private companies exist, insufficient overlap between same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable companies (fewer than 3 datapoints at similar valuation tier) would introduce undue estimation error.

The triangulated range is $1.7B (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median point of $2.0B. This range reflects stage-appropriate capital deployment patterns but carries material uncertainty owing to reliance on a single method and the absence of sufficient narrow-peer validation. Key uncertainties center on technology-stage maturity relative to peers, capital intensity trajectory, and the broader nuclear sector's deployment timeline—variables not captured in the quantitative data available.
